explanation: The natural language property states that 'Exceeding sensor limits' (represented by the atomic proposition 'limits') shall 'latch an autopilot pullup' (represented by 'pullup'). This action is conditional on 'the pilot is not in control' (which is '!standby' since 'standby' means pilot is in control) AND 'the system is supported without failures' (which is 'supported & !apfail' since 'apfail' indicates failure). Combining these conditions, the trigger for the latching action is 'limits & !standby & supported & !apfail'. The term 'latch' implies that once 'pullup' becomes true due to this trigger, it remains true indefinitely. The LTL template G (bool_exp1 -> (G bool_exp3)) precisely captures this requirement: globally, whenever the trigger condition (bool_exp1) is met, the latched state (bool_exp3) must hold true from that point onwards forever. Therefore, bool_exp1 is set to 'limits & !standby & supported & !apfail', and bool_exp3 is set to 'pullup'.

This property captures that upon the rising edge of the condition 'exceeding sensor limits' (bool_exp1) AND 'pilot not in control and system supported without failures' (bool_exp2), an autopilot pullup (bool_exp3) must be latched, meaning it will hold true from the next state for N_DURATION time steps or indefinitely. It also ensures that if the combined condition is already true, pullup is already in a latched state.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G (((! (bool_exp1 & bool_exp2)) & (X (bool_exp1 & bool_exp2))) -> (X ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))))) & ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))))

It also dictates that if the condition is already active, pullup must already be true and remain so indefinitely.

This applies whenever the conditions are met.
